description: Knowledge and application of relevant technologies, tools, and best practices.

signals: I actively learn and apply the technologies, tools, and practices defined by the team
I seek guidance to follow existing implementations and patterns
examples: I created new REST APIs by following an existing implementation and best practices
I actively learn from teammates and incorporate feedback on technology choices

signals: I become the go-to person for one or more technologies, tools, or methodologies
I proactively expand my expertise beyond immediate tasks
examples: I have in-depth knowledge of the OAuth protocol and best authentication practices
I documented and shared patterns that became team standards

signals: I research, prototype, and introduce new technologies, tools, or practices that improve the team's effectiveness
I balance experimentation with reliable delivery
examples: After detailed research, I successfully introduced AlloyDB to improve our database scalability
I piloted a feature flag library and rolled it out with a clear migration plan

signals: I deeply understand the entire technical ecosystem, including architecture, tools, and best practices
I drive improvements across the team and provide guidance on complex technical decisions
examples: I provide guidance on Kotlin, CI/CD best practices, or testing strategies to enhance system reliability
I led pairing sessions to improve testing strategies across the team

signals: I design and develop new technologies, tools, or practices that significantly improve engineering effectiveness
My contributions are widely adopted and improve quality across multiple teams
examples: I developed a new integration testing framework that streamlined automated testing across multiple teams
I created a standardized observability pattern that became the company standard
